key_insight: A self-attention neural surrogate can learn PFEM free-surface fluid dynamics directly on evolving meshes, enabling accurate long-horizon prediction while preserving geometric fidelity.
engineering_relevance: This offers a scalable reduced-order alternative to expensive free-surface CFD simulations, including non-Newtonian flows and postprocessed stress reconstruction for engineering analysis.
method: Mesh-preserving self-attention on PFEM nodes to model spatial interactions
Two variants: standard self-attention and a cheaper linear attention version
Supports remeshing/node redistribution during rollout to maintain mesh quality
evidence_signal: Evaluated on 2D and 3D free-surface benchmarks with evolving geometry and varying material parameters, showing accurate transient and final-state predictions with improved scalability.
watch_item: Performance still depends on robust mesh evolution and training data coverage, so instability may appear when extrapolating to unseen geometries or rheologies.
paper_title: Attention mechanism for scalable mesh-based neural surrogates of free-surface fluids
